item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a +1 from me, I love serious articles with funny/absurd/stupid-at-first-glance premises. With so many SCP's already existing it's starting to get hard to think of something original, so when people think outside of the box like this it leads to really cool things. Great job :))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
those footnotes have got to be some of the longest i've ever seen in a non-format-screw article. any way to either trim those down further or integrate more of their content into the main article?

as for the content itself, i'm still ruminating on my vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really minor issue, but at least on my phone the line spacing of the last footnote gets larger after the line break.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is phenomenally goofy as a concept, which made me want to take it less seriously at first glance. However, the actual layout of the article and execution of bumblebee flight as an actual arcane ritual strayed serious enough (while still remaining fabulously weird) that I ended up really enjoying it.

item.title: According to all known laws of aviation...
item.content:encoded:
... there is no way that a bee should be able to fly.

Its wings are too small to get its fat little body off the ground.

The bee, of course, flies anyways.

Because bees don't care what humans think is impossible.

A serious take on a stupid premise. I choose this number because it's "BEES" upside-down. That also might be the reason I wrote it in the first place.

The Da'as Elyon subclass denotes an anomaly that cannot be physically contained, so the public must be convinced it isn't anomalous.

Thanks to Cerastes and company for feedback!

Image source.

Discover more abject stupidity in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
